title: Been looking into automated trading bots for a while to see slow growth
author: whosjkt
selftext:
Finally decided to test one properly instead of jumping between random signal groups and overleveraged stuff.

Started using this thing called Terminal.3M a few days ago in dry-run mode first before risking any real money. What caught my attention was it’s apparently built by an ex-LME metals trader and focuses more on execution + risk management rather than “1000% gain” marketing.

So far it seems more like infrastructure than a typical Telegram signal bot:  
cloud based  
runs on Hyperliquid  
scans multiple markets at once  
sends alerts + logs everything  
rule-based entries instead of discretionary calls

Not expecting miracles obviously, just looking for something consistent and emotionless over time rather than chasing big wins.  
Anyone else here tried automated systems long term? Curious what people look for when deciding if a system is actually legit or not.
